picks.title: De Parade
picks.blurb: The world's only travelling theatre festival pitches its fairground of tents — 95+ makers of theatre, dance, mime and music plus food stalls and carousel-lit ev
picks.why: Travelling theatre fairground · once a year
picks.source: Fresh find · Your Little Black Book · I amsterdam

picks.title: IJ-Hallen Flea Market
picks.blurb: <cite index="11-5">One of Europe's largest second-hand markets at NDSM Wharf with food & local producers</cite>.
picks.source: I amsterdam · AmsterdamTips · Your Little Black Book, AmsterdamTips · Your Little Black Book

picks.title: World Press Photo Exhibition 2026
picks.blurb: Annual showcase of world's most compelling photojournalism capturing contemporary history and global events through award-winning images.
picks.source: I amsterdam · Your Little Black Book

picks.title: Milkshake Festival
picks.blurb: Open-minded, gloriously dressed-up dance festival mixing house, disco, pop, techno and dancehall with drag and voguing — kicking off Pride week.
picks.source: Fresh find · Your Little Black Book · Songkick · I amsterdam

picks.title: Kwaku Summer Festival
picks.blurb: Vibrant festival with Surinamese cuisine, Caribbean specialities, live music, dance and family activities.
picks.source: Your Little Black Book · I amsterdam

picks.title: Ekō – Japan in twee beeldverhalen
picks.blurb: Japanese art and cultural narratives told through two distinct visual stories at Amsterdam's maritime museum.

picks.title: Nederland – Japan. Door de tijd verbonden
picks.blurb: Explore centuries of Dutch-Japanese cultural exchange through rare objects, lacquerware, swords, and decorative screens.
picks.why: Stunning Japan exhibition running all weekend

picks.title: Martine Gutierrez – Wunderkind
picks.blurb: Artist explores identity and self-expression through photographs, self-portraits, childhood toys, and handmade objects from personal discovery.
picks.why: Intimate exploration of identity and creativity

picks.title: The Little Big Things
picks.blurb: West End musical based on true story of Henry Fraser, showcasing resilience, humor and love after life-changing moment.

picks.title: WorldPride Amsterdam
picks.blurb: Amsterdam hosts WorldPride 2026: two weeks of citywide street parties and culture, crowned by the world-famous Canal Parade through the grachten.

picks.title: The Maker Market
picks.blurb:
New editions, discover new creators!
 The Maker Market takes place monthly at De Hallen in Amsterdam-West, a vibrant hub of craftsmanship, design, and inspirati
picks.source: I amsterdam · AmsterdamTips · I amsterdam, Your Little Black Book

picks.title: American Identities
picks.blurb:
David Levinthal 
 David Levinthal (born 1949) explores the role of myths in American visual culture. Using carefully constructed miniature scenes, the artist re

picks.title: Chef's Evening
picks.blurb:
Dining in a unique setting
 Sharing is connecting. The dishes are festively presented on large platters, so that you enjoy pure flavors together with your table

picks.title: STRAAT Collectie
picks.venue: STRAAT Museum – Street art en graffiti in Amsterdam
picks.blurb:
Museum for street art and graffiti
 We focus on curating a collection that truly reflects the international street art movement. We do this by recruiting artist

picks.title: Bottomless Brunch
picks.blurb:
Bottomless brunch
 For groups larger than 10 people, there is a special offer available. This deal is valid at Cut Throat's location at Beursplein 5. The highli

picks.title: Vondelpark Openluchttheater
picks.blurb:
In the heart of Amsterdam
 The first real Vondelpark festivals originated in the 1950s, and this continued to grow. Since 1974, the Vondelpark Open Air Theatre

picks.title: De Roode Remise Singing Session
picks.blurb:
De Roode Remise Singing Session
 The core singers know each other from the famous Backroom Sessions at Mulligan's Irish Bar, but everyone, experienced or inexpe

picks.title: Nicolas van Poucke - Pianorecital
picks.blurb:
Master pianist Nicolas van Poucke
 Master pianist Nicolas van Poucke returns to the Kleine Zaal, where he continues his remarkable recital tradition. Praised by

picks.title: VriendenLoterij Summer Concerts
picks.blurb:
Celebrate summer with music
 As in previous years, you can once again enjoy epic film music, jazz, singer songwriter, close harmony and much more. Who or what w

picks.title: Exhibition Worn in the Rijksmuseum
picks.blurb:
WORN shows how clothing can lead a life of its own
 In this presentation, you discover worn, repaired and altered pieces from the collection, dating from 1640 t

picks.title: Lunchtime organ concerts at Waterlooplein
picks.blurb:
A moment of music in the middle of the day
 Step inside the Moses and Aaron Church and leave the hustle and bustle of the city behind for a moment. These luncht

picks.title: Ligconcert® presents: lying down concert at the Maritime Museum
picks.blurb:
10,000 sparkling lights
 The one and only Ligconcert® at the Maritime Museum Amsterdam under the enchanting glass roof, illuminated by no fewer than 10,000 spar

picks.title: BRET x Off World - Olivia Lensen & Tsepo
picks.blurb: OLIVIA LENSEN, Tsepo at BRET.

picks.title: Het Scheepvaartmuseum – Summer Activities
picks.blurb: Maritime museum offering water yoga, plastic fishing, storytelling, ship exploration, and marine discovery activities; under-5s enter free.
picks.why: Free entry for youngest; summer activities galore

picks.title: Hortus Botanicus
picks.blurb: One of the world’s oldest botanical gardens — a butterfly greenhouse and three-climate glasshouse to wander.
picks.source: De Hortus · Your Little Black Book

picks.title: Cinema Culinair
picks.blurb: Watch food-themed films while dining on dishes inspired by or matching the on-screen menu exactly.

picks.title: Pllek — waterfront hang
picks.blurb: A cargo-container café-bar on the NDSM wharf — big windows onto the IJ, sofas, and a wood stove when it turns.
picks.why: Indoor seating · IJ views · cozy when grey

picks.title: Café Chris — oldest brown café
picks.blurb: Open since 1624: a creaky, golden-lit Jordaan brown café for a slow afternoon beer while it pours outside.

picks.title: Foodhallen
picks.blurb: An indoor food hall in a converted tram depot — 20-odd stalls, long tables and two bars under one roof.
picks.why: Indoor · grazing for a crowd · weather-proof

picks.title: Sauna Deco — Art Deco baths
picks.blurb: A 1920s Art Deco bathhouse (salvaged from a Paris department store) with saunas, steam and a quiet tea room.
picks.why: Warm · restorative · the ultimate cold-and-wet antidote

picks.title: Vondelpark playgrounds
picks.blurb: Amsterdam’s favourite park: open-air playgrounds, the Kinderkookkafé, ducks and room to run.
picks.why: Free · easy any time with kids

picks.title: The Movies — arthouse cinema
picks.blurb: The Netherlands’ oldest cinema still running — Art Deco rooms, a dinner-and-a-film deal, arthouse bills.
picks.why: Indoor · dinner + a film · perfect when it’s wet

picks.title: Two Story
picks.venue: Two Story — gallery, design store & café
picks.blurb: An 18th-century canal house rebuilt by Copenhagen's Atelier Axo: design store up front, courtyard café, gallery rotating every three months.
picks.why: Copenhagen design eye, Amsterdam canal house

picks.title: Foam Talent 2026
picks.blurb: Fifteen emerging photographers from 107 countries — fresh on its launch weekend.
picks.why: New this week · indoor · quick + sharp

picks.title: Ivna Esajas — Wayward Lines
picks.blurb: The ABN Amro Art Award show threads diasporic memory through installation — its very last weekend.
picks.why: Closes Sunday · indoor · see it or miss it

picks.title: Dekmantel Festival
picks.blurb: Peerless underground electronic festival in the forest — the sharpest techno, house and experimental line-up of the year.

picks.title: Foam
picks.blurb: A nimble photography museum in a canal house — big-name retrospectives and emerging talent, always worth the drop-in.

picks.title: Droog
picks.blurb: Where Dutch conceptual design was born: gallery, shop, courtyard garden and a single hotel room in a Staalstraat warehouse.

picks.title: MENDO
picks.blurb: A self-described candy store for book aficionados: black-walled cabinet of photography, design and fashion monographs.

picks.title: Moise
picks.blurb: An understated De Pijp boutique with a razor-sharp eye — Acne, Lemaire, Marant and the like, hand-picked and beautifully shown.

picks.title: Patta
picks.blurb: The homegrown streetwear label and culture hub — drops, sneakers and the community that put Amsterdam on the map.

picks.title: X Bank
picks.blurb: A concept store in a former bank showing 180+ Dutch fashion, design and art labels — couture to hand-thrown ceramics.
picks.why: Best of Dutch design · one room

picks.title: Melkweg
picks.blurb: A former dairy turned multi-room culture machine — live music, club nights, film and a gallery, all under one roof.

picks.title: Mobilia
picks.blurb: Three floors of high design on the Utrechtsestraat — icons by the masters alongside more attainable covetables.

picks.title: Utrecht
picks.blurb: Amsterdam’s quieter little sister — wharf-side canals, the soaring Dom Tower and a real café culture, minus the crowds.

picks.title: Vlieger
picks.blurb: A two-floor temple to paper since 1869 — hundreds of colours, handmade sheets and card stacked to the ceiling.

picks.title: Efteling
picks.blurb: Europe’s oldest fairytale theme park — enchanted forest, dark rides and proper coasters in a storybook world.

picks.title: IJ-Hallen
picks.blurb: Europe’s biggest flea market — 750 stalls across two NDSM shipyard warehouses, a proper treasure-hunt of vintage and junk.

picks.title: Micropia
picks.blurb: The world’s only microbe museum — microscopes, a kiss-o-meter and the tiny life all around us.

picks.title: Paradiso
picks.blurb: Amsterdam’s legendary music hall in a converted church — stained glass overhead, a heaving floor below, indie to techno.

picks.title: Kriterion
picks.blurb: A legendary student-run cinema and café since 1945 — arthouse programming with a buzzy terrace.

picks.title: Madurodam
picks.blurb: The whole of the Netherlands at 1:25 — miniature canals, Schiphol and windmills kids can tower over.

picks.title: Nxt Museum
picks.blurb: Warehouse-scale new-media museum in Noord: room-sized audiovisual installations rewired each season by artists and coders.

picks.title: Martin Parr: Very Modern and Rather Ugly
picks.blurb: Retrospective of the British photographer’s satirical, affectionate eye on modern life and consumer culture — chip queues, deck chairs and all.

picks.title: Muiderslot
picks.blurb: A moated medieval castle on the IJmeer — turrets, falconry and a walled garden, the best-preserved fortress in the country.

picks.title: TonTon Club
picks.blurb: An arcade bar where you pay to play — pinball, classic cabinets and Japanese games, with beer and gyoza on tap.

picks.title: Dappermarkt
picks.blurb: A buzzing, multicultural Oost street market — spices, textiles and world food at honest neighbourhood prices.

picks.title: Garage Noord
picks.blurb: A club, kitchen and cultural space in a former garage — daytime plates, late-night dancing.
picks.why: Kitchen by day · club by night

picks.title: Rijksmuseum
picks.blurb: The Netherlands’ treasure house — Rembrandt’s Night Watch, Vermeer and the Golden Age masters in a cathedral of a building.
picks.why: The Night Watch · the big one

picks.title: Bloemenmarkt
picks.blurb: The world’s only floating flower market — barges of tulips, bulbs and seeds moored along the Singel since 1862.

picks.title: De Filmhallen
picks.blurb: A nine-screen arthouse cinema in a converted tram depot — with a bustling food hall right next door.
picks.why: Arthouse · in the old tram depot

picks.title: Movies at H'ART — open-air cinema
picks.blurb: The museum courtyard turns open-air cinema for three August weeks — Cinema Paradiso, Blade Runner, Barbie at sunset, rain or shine.
picks.why: Sunset films at the museum — sells out

picks.title: Wild swimming: Gaasperplas & co
picks.blurb: Three real swimming lakes within cycling distance — grass banks, clean water, zero entrance fee. Where the city actually cools off.

picks.title: ARTIS-Aquarium
picks.blurb: The 1882 aquarium building, newly restored: glowing tanks, coral rooms and an underwater Amsterdam canal — the rainy-day ace of the Plantage.

picks.title: ARTIS Royal Zoo
picks.blurb: The country’s oldest zoo — big cats, an aquarium and a planetarium across a leafy 19th-century park.
picks.why: All-day with kids · indoor + outdoor mix

picks.title: Eye Filmmuseum — repertory screening
picks.blurb: Across the IJ by free ferry: a striking film museum with a daily repertory programme and a river-view café.
picks.why: Indoor · a film + the ferry ride · weather-proof

picks.title: Sexyland World
picks.blurb: A members’ club owned by 1,000 people — a different host and a different vibe every single night.

picks.title: Wils at the Farm
picks.blurb: Joris Bijdendijk and Thomas Val cook open-fire dinners at the farm that grows their ingredients — long tables, meadow views.
picks.why: Star kitchen relocated to its farm

picks.title: Marken & Volendam
picks.blurb: Two former Zuiderzee fishing villages — wooden houses, working harbours and smoked eel, an easy half-day from Centraal.

picks.title: Zandvoort Beach
picks.blurb: The nearest North Sea beach — wide sands, beach clubs and a direct train from Centraal; in summer half the city decamps here.

picks.title: Albert Cuypmarkt
picks.blurb: The Netherlands’ biggest street market — 250-odd stalls of stroopwafels, cheese, fabric and flowers the length of De Pijp.

picks.title: Royal Concertgebouw
picks.blurb: One of the world’s great concert halls — peerless acoustics, a resident world-class orchestra, and free Wednesday lunch concerts.

picks.title: Waterlooplein Flea Market
picks.blurb: The city’s oldest flea market — secondhand everything, vintage denim, records and bric-a-brac spread out beside the Stopera.

picks.title: The Jollof Club West-African restaurant
picks.blurb: Chef Nicolas Aquaa's modern West-African cuisine inspired by Ghana, Nigeria and Senegal with wines and cocktails.
picks.why: Innovative West-African cooking with culture and storytellin

picks.title: American Myth Memory – David Levinthal Photographs
picks.blurb: Photographic exploration of American cultural mythology and collective memory through contemporary artistic vision.
picks.why: Contemporary American photography and cultural critique

picks.title: Nara Nara Egyptian restaurant
picks.blurb: Chef Said's Egyptian restaurant with Cairo-inspired shared dishes, Mediterranean touches and authentic flavours.
picks.why: Authentic Egyptian cuisine perfect for family sharing

picks.title: BAK
picks.blurb: A light-filled warehouse with an open kitchen and an art-covered wall; a set seasonal menu of produce-driven dishes.

picks.title: Museum Van Loon
picks.blurb: A patrician canal house still furnished as lived: portraits, coach house, and the quietest formal garden inside the ring.

picks.title: Pluk de Nacht Film Festival
picks.blurb: 20+ year tradition: rare unreleased films on big screen, beach chairs, blankets, campfire, cocktails by the IJ.
picks.why: Curated cinema festival with unique films; iconic summer exp

picks.title: Kaap Amsterdam
picks.blurb: Urban beach with free water views, safe swimming, Woodstock vibes, specialty sandwiches, and relaxed setting.
picks.why: Safe swimming spot with high vacation feel

picks.title: Strand Zuid
picks.blurb: One of Amsterdam's first beach clubs with sand, sun loungers, cold drinks, and boat access.
picks.why: Original beach club with proper sand experience

picks.title: Hannekes Boom
picks.blurb: Boat-accessible beach club with swimming, colorful interior, and relaxed waterfront atmosphere.
picks.why: Scenic waterside escape with boating access

picks.title: Mokumboot: sloepje huren
picks.blurb: Rent a small boat and explore Amsterdam's canals at your own pace—perfect for family water adventures.
picks.why: Classic summer activity on the water
